quote: FY2025 cost of revenue surged 23% YoY to RMB 188.8B, driven by fulfillment, bandwidth/server, and payment processing fees: FY2025 CoR: RMB 188,801.8M (+23% from RMB 153,900.4M in FY2024). Gross margin compressed to 56.3% from 60.9% in FY2024. The increase was primarily driven by increased fulfillment fees, bandwidth and server costs, and payment processing fees.

FY2025 operating expenses grew 13% but R&D surged 30%, signaling strategic technology investment: FY2025 opex breakdown: S&M RMB 125,287.9M (+13%), G&A RMB 6,635.8M (-12%), R&D RMB 16,496.2M (+30%). Total opex RMB 148,419.9M (+13%). R&D increase due to staff-related costs and bandwidth/server costs.

Q4 2025 operating profit grew +8.3% YoY despite FY2025 declining -13%, suggesting margin compression may be bottoming: Q4 2025 operating profit: RMB 27,719.7M vs Q4 2024 RMB 25,592.2M (+8.3%). Q4 operating margin: 22.4% vs Q4 2024 23.1%. FY2025 operating profit RMB 94,624.1M vs FY2024 RMB 108,422.9M (-13%). FY2025 operating margin: 21.9% vs FY2024 27.5%.

S&M expense as % of revenue remained stable at ~29% in FY2025, indicating disciplined marketing spend despite investment cycle: FY2025 S&M/Revenue: 125,287.9/431,845.7 = 29.0%. FY2024 S&M/Revenue: 111,300.5/393,836.1 = 28.3%. Q4 2025 S&M/Revenue: 34,352.3/123,912.2 = 27.7%. Slight increase but not dramatic escalation.

PDD earned RMB 25.6B in interest & investment income in FY2025, up 24% YoY, representing a hidden profit engine: FY2025 interest & investment income: RMB 25,583.8M (US$3,659.4M). FY2024: RMB 20,553.5M. Growth: +24.5% YoY. This is 27.0% of FY2025 operating profit (RMB 94,624.1M) and 25.7% of FY2025 net income (RMB 99,364.5M).

PDD's cash + short-term investments reached RMB 422.3B ($60.4B) at year-end 2025, plus RMB 73.8B restricted cash and RMB 104.7B non-current assets (time deposits, debt securities): Dec 31 2025: Cash & equivalents RMB 108,900.6M. Short-term investments RMB 313,407.7M. Restricted cash RMB 73,830.8M. Other non-current assets RMB 104,707.7M (mainly time deposits, held-to-maturity debt securities, available-for-sale debt securities). Total liquid assets ~RMB 600.8B.

Implied yield on total liquid assets is approximately 4.3%, consistent with Chinese money market/treasury rates: FY2025 interest & investment income RMB 25,583.8M. Average liquid assets (FY2024 end: 331.6+68.4+83.4=483.4B; FY2025 end: 422.3+73.8+104.7=600.8B; average ~542.1B). Implied yield: 25.6/542.1 = ~4.7%. This is consistent with 1-year Chinese treasury deposit rates of 3.5-5.0%.

Interest income alone explains why FY2025 net income (RMB 99.4B) exceeded operating profit (RMB 94.6B) despite FX losses: FY2025: Operating profit RMB 94,624.1M. Interest & investment income +RMB 25,583.8M. Other income +RMB 2,726.9M. FX loss -RMB 1,966.6M. Net income RMB 99,364.5M. Interest income more than offset the RMB 13.8B operating profit decline.

Co-CEO Jiazhen Zhao: '2026 marks the beginning of our journey into the next decade. Going into this new chapter, supply chain investment is where we will place our greatest conviction. We are committed to dedicating significant resources, with an all-in mindset, to drive lasting benefits to the entire ecosystem.' VP Finance Jun Liu: 'These investments are firm and long-term, and will inevitably affect our financial performance.'

The 'Hundred Billion Support Program' was launched in early 2025. Key components: RMB 10B fee reduction program, logistics support initiative, new quality merchant support. Impact: FY2025 operating profit declined to RMB 94.6B from RMB 108.4B (-13%). Zhao described 2025 as 'the year in which we made our largest investment in high-quality development.' Program will continue in 2026.

Q4 2025 operating profit grew +8.3% YoY, suggesting the worst of margin compression may be over — or Q4 was seasonally strong: Q4 2025 operating profit RMB 27.7B vs Q4 2024 RMB 25.6B (+8.3%). Quarterly operating profit trajectory in 2025: Q1 RMB 16.1B (-38% YoY), Q2 RMB 25.8B (-21% YoY), Q3 RMB 25.0B (+2% YoY), Q4 RMB 27.7B (+8.3% YoY). Clear improving trend through the year.

PDD has NO fintech business, unlike Alibaba (Ant Group) and JD.com (JD Finance) — this is a notable absence given its cash pile and merchant base: PDD Holdings does not operate a publicly disclosed fintech platform comparable to Alibaba's Ant Group or JD.com's JD Finance. No merchant lending, consumer credit, or digital banking products are disclosed. Transaction services revenue appears limited to payment processing and logistics fees. This means PDD's ~RMB 600B in liquid assets earn interest income but do not support a lending business that could generate much higher returns.

PDD has the highest gross margin (56-61%) and operating margin (22-28%) among Chinese e-commerce peers, reflecting pure marketplace leverage: FY2025 comparison: PDD gross margin 56.3% (down from 60.9%), operating margin 21.9% (down from 27.5%). Alibaba FY2025 (ended Mar 2025): gross margin ~39.9%, operating margin ~14-15%. JD.com: gross margin ~15.9% (Q2 2025), operating margin ~2.6% (TTM). PDD's margins are structurally superior due to pure 3P marketplace + C2M model.

PDD trades at lowest P/E multiple (~10x) despite highest margins, reflecting market discount for investment cycle uncertainty and geopolitical risk: Forward P/E multiples (March 2026): PDD ~10x, JD.com ~9-10x, Alibaba ~19x. PDD has highest profitability (net margin ~23%) but trades at discount to Alibaba due to: (1) investment cycle uncertainty, (2) Temu tariff/regulatory risk, (3) no capital return program (no buyback, no dividend).

Alibaba Q4 FY2025 (Mar 2025 quarter): Customer management revenue grew 12% YoY, driven by improvement of take rate from increasing penetration of Quanzhantui and addition of 0.6% software service fees. Taobao Tmall Group revenue reached RMB 101.4B in Q4 FY2025, up 9%.

JD.com's 1P model generates highest absolute revenue (~$187B) but lowest margins (~2.6% operating); growing 3P mix is the margin story: JD.com FY2025 annual revenue ~$187.2B (+17.9% YoY). JD Retail operating margin improved to 4.5% (Q2) and 5.9% (Q3). Consolidated operating margin ~2.6% dragged by new business investments. JD's take rate comparison is not directly applicable due to 1P gross revenue recognition vs PDD/BABA net recognition.

Pinduoduo domestic is the profit engine; Temu international is the investment engine — but TS revenue convergence makes this harder to separate: Revenue mix FY2025: OMS RMB 217.8B (50.4%) vs TS RMB 214.1B (49.6%). OMS is primarily Pinduoduo domestic advertising (high margin, ~70%+ gross margin on ad revenue). TS includes both Pinduoduo transaction fees AND Temu's gross revenue (much lower margin due to fulfillment costs). The 23% CoR increase (RMB 188.8B) is substantially driven by Temu fulfillment. PDD does not break out Pinduoduo vs Temu profitability.

FY2025 gross margin compressed 460bps to 56.3%, but this still exceeds most global e-commerce platforms and reflects marketplace model strength: FY2025 gross margin: (431,845.7 - 188,801.8) / 431,845.7 = 56.3%. FY2024: (393,836.1 - 153,900.4) / 393,836.1 = 60.9%. FY2023: ~63%. Compression is primarily Temu fulfillment costs flowing through CoR. For comparison: Amazon gross margin ~48%, Alibaba ~40%, JD ~16%, MercadoLibre ~53%.

Business model quality thesis: PDD earns ~RMB 120B from high-quality sources (OMS ads + interest income) and uses ~RMB 25B+ to subsidize Temu growth: High-quality earnings: OMS revenue RMB 217.8B at estimated ~50% operating margin = ~RMB 109B operating contribution. Interest & investment income: RMB 25.6B. Total high-quality earnings ~RMB 134B. Actual consolidated operating profit: RMB 94.6B. Implied Temu + merchant program drag: ~RMB 40B. This suggests Pinduoduo domestic + interest income alone generates ~RMB 134B in operating income, while Temu operations and the 100B merchant program consume ~RMB 40B.

Cash generation remains strong despite investment cycle: FY2025 OCF RMB 106.9B, cash pile grew RMB 90.7B YoY to RMB 422.3B: FY2025 operating cash flow: RMB 106,938.7M (down from RMB 121,929.3M in FY2024, -12.3%). Cash + ST investments: RMB 422,308.3M at Dec 2025 vs RMB 331,560.0M at Dec 2024, growth of RMB 90.7B (+27.4%). Even during the investment cycle, PDD is accumulating cash at an extraordinary rate.

PDD FY2025 revenue reached RMB 431.8B (+10% YoY), with OMS and TS near parity for the first time: FY2025: Online marketing services RMB 217,783.0M (+10% YoY from RMB 197,934.2M). Transaction services RMB 214,062.7M (+9% YoY from RMB 195,901.9M). Total revenue RMB 431,845.7M (+10% YoY from RMB 393,836.1M). Revenue mix: OMS 50.4%, TS 49.6%.

Q4 2025 showed divergent growth: OMS decelerated to +5% while TS accelerated to +19%, signaling Temu-driven transaction growth: Q4 2025: OMS RMB 60,010.1M (+5% YoY from RMB 57,011.1M). TS RMB 63,902.1M (+19% YoY from RMB 53,599.0M). Total Q4 revenue RMB 123,912.2M (+12% YoY).

Quarterly OMS growth trajectory shows deceleration through 2025: Q1 +15%, Q2 +13%, Q3 +8%, Q4 +5%: Q1 2025 OMS growth +15%. Q2 2025 OMS growth +13%. Q3 2025 OMS RMB 53,300M (+8% YoY). Q4 2025 OMS RMB 60,010.1M (+5% YoY). This sequential deceleration suggests domestic ad monetization is approaching maturity.

Revenue mix shifted from OMS-dominant to near-parity in 2 years, reflecting Temu's structural impact: FY2023: OMS was ~66% of revenue, TS ~34%. FY2024: OMS 50.3%, TS 49.7%. FY2025: OMS 50.4%, TS 49.6%. The shift from 2:1 OMS:TS ratio to 1:1 reflects Temu's transaction-based model contributing growing share.

PDD's blended take rate for FY2025 is approximately 4.8-5.4% depending on GMV estimate used, up from ~3.6% in FY2021: FY2025 revenue: RMB 431.8B. Analyst GMV estimates for PDD consolidated: RMB 8-9T range (domestic ~RMB 5T + Temu ~RMB 500-650B in USD terms = ~RMB 3.5-4.6T equivalent). Using domestic GMV ~RMB 5T (Bernstein Q1 2025 domestic GMV growth 17-18% applied to ~RMB 4.05T 2023 base implies ~RMB 5.5T in 2025) + Temu ~$70B (~RMB 500B): Total GMV ~RMB 6T. Take rate = 431.8/6,000 = ~7.2%. However, this is misleading because Temu revenue is recorded gross. Adjusting for Temu gross vs net: Pinduoduo domestic take rate = OMS revenue / domestic GMV = 217.8B / 5,500B = ~4.0%.

Bernstein estimates ~35% of GMV for Temu under fully managed model, ~40% under fully managed in some estimates, and low double digits under semi-managed model. In US market, composition is approximately 60% fully managed, 30% semi-managed, 10% local-to-local as of H1 2025.

OMS quarterly growth trajectory in 2025: Q1 +15%, Q2 +13%, Q3 +8%, Q4 +5%. Bernstein SocGen downgraded PDD citing 'maturing domestic business' with 'recent non-growth in daily active users and time spent as problematic for a business model reliant on top funnel dominance.' Analyst Robin Zhu flagged this concern.
TipRanks reports PDD Holdings is scheduled to report earnings on May 22, 2026, After Close (Confirmed), for the Q1 2026 period.

The European Council agreed to apply a fixed customs duty of EUR 3 on small parcels valued at less than EUR 150 entering the EU from July 1, 2026. According to the European Commission, around 4.6 billion shipments valued under EUR 150 entered the EU in 2024, with around 91% originating from China. The measure covers ~93% of all e-commerce flows into the EU.

France, Romania, Netherlands already imposed national fees (EUR 2-5) on low-value parcels starting Jan-Feb 2026, ahead of EU-wide July 1 deadline: France introduced a EUR 2 fee raised to EUR 5, effective Jan 1, 2026. Romania introduced ~EUR 5 logistics fee, effective Jan 1, 2026. Netherlands has a EUR 2 fee proposal for Feb 1, 2026.

Three Chinese agencies — NDRC, SAMR, and CAC — jointly issued pricing regulations for internet platforms on Dec 9, 2025, taking effect April 10, 2026. Rules ban platform operators from leveraging dominant scale to impose 'lowest price' agreements, prohibit using traffic throttling or algorithm penalties to pressure merchants, and forbid algorithmic price discrimination.

Small and medium-sized merchants may benefit from clearer rules that prevent larger platforms from forcing disadvantageous pricing tactics. By curbing 'involution-style' price wars and algorithmic exploitation, the rules intend to shift competition toward quality, innovation, and service differentiation.

US-China trade truce: Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs Feb 2026; effective rate on Chinese goods ~30%; Trump-Xi meetings led to tariff suspensions: On Feb 20, 2026, the Supreme Court ruled IEEPA cannot be used for tariffs. Trump imposed 10% global tariff under Section 122 for 150 days. Effective tariff rate on Chinese goods remains ~30%. Trump-Xi meetings resulted in agreement to reduce tariffs — Washington suspended 24% for one year, China suspended counter-tariffs and rare earth restrictions.

The US de minimis exemption allowing shipments valued at $800 or less to enter duty-free was removed on 29 August 2025. When the de minimis loophole was revoked in April 2025, Temu rapidly shifted strategy, promoting US-based inventory through 'local' badges and expanding warehouse capacity domestically.

Temu semi-managed model expected to contribute over $10B in revenue; local seller program active in 30+ markets with Shopify integration: The semi-managed model is expected to contribute over $10 billion, nearly matching Temu's total 2023 sales. Local Seller Program now active in 30+ markets. Temu launched Shopify app enabling merchants to manage listings, inventory, and fulfillment directly through Shopify in 30+ markets.

PDD has made zero M&A moves despite $58B+ cash pile; analysts speculate on logistics acquisitions but company is building organically: PDD Holdings has made no investments or acquisitions according to Tracxn tracking. Analysts speculate PDD may acquire struggling retail brands or build global logistics rivaling FedEx/DHL. Current strategy appears focused on organic build: R&D spending hit record in 2025, deploying sorting robotics in US/EU hubs.

Analyst consensus: Buy rating, average price target $137-146 vs current $106; Goldman Sachs at $147, Barclays at $165: 11 analysts give Buy consensus with $137.55 target. 19 analysts give $146.16 consensus. Goldman Sachs Buy at $147, Barclays Buy at $165, HSBC raised to $155, Macquarie raised to $165.
'Smart money' is moving: Dodge & Cox and Hillhouse (HHLR) significantly increased their positions in late 2025, signaling that the valuation (currently at Forward P/E of ~9x) is too low to ignore.

The Trump Administration delayed some trade actions in fall 2025 as part of efforts to seek a trade deal ahead of President Trump's planned visit to China in late March 2026. USTR extended tariff exclusions on 178 Chinese products until November 10, 2026.

USTR launched two Section 301 investigations into excess capacity and forced labor targeting China, in a move to find alternative means of imposing import duties following the Supreme Court's IEEPA ruling. Hearings will be held in April and May.

Alibaba Q4 2025 revenue grew 7% (10% ex-SunART/InTime); Taobao/Tmall grew 6% but faces fierce price competition: Alibaba demonstrated solid performance in Q4 2025, with a 7% increase in total consolidated revenue. Excluding SunART and InTime, group revenue grew by 10% YoY. Non-GAAP net income reached 29.8 billion RMB, up 22%. Taobao and Tmall reported 6% YoY growth but face fierce competition from Pinduoduo and Douyin.

Alibaba Cloud experienced a 36% year-over-year revenue growth in Q4 2025, primarily driven by demand for AI-related services, with AI workloads growing at triple-digit rates for ten consecutive quarters.

In 2025, quick commerce weighed heavily on profitability due to high fulfillment costs, small basket sizes, and intense competition. The commerce division's adjusted EBITA for the half year ended Sept. 30, 2025, fell 47% year over year, due to heavy investment in quick commerce.

JD Takeaway has ~5% food delivery market share, targeting 10% by 2026; Meituan dominates at 64-67%: JD Takeaway has inched toward 5% of the market. This faces challenges from Meituan's 64-67% dominance. Investors are watching whether JD Takeaway can sustain its 5% share and grow it to 10% by 2026.

JD Takeaway could cost $4.8B in losses for 2025, wiping out 36% of JD's operating profit: Analysts estimate the segment could incur RMB34 billion ($4.8 billion) in losses for 2025, wiping out 36% of JD.com's parent company's operating profit.

Douyin e-commerce GMV hit ~3.5T yuan in 2024 (+30% YoY), targeting 4.2T in 2025; first 10 months of 2025 exceeded 30% growth: Douyin E-commerce's GMV in 2024 was approximately 3.5 trillion yuan (+30% YoY). 2025 target was 4.2 trillion yuan. For the first ten months of 2025, growth exceeded 30%, with annual GMV expected to surpass 4 trillion yuan.

Douyin shelf e-commerce grew 49% YoY and now contributes ~50% of GMV (up from 30% in 2023, 40% in 2024): Shelf e-commerce contributed 30% of GMV in 2023, 40% in 2024, and appears to have reached 50% in 2025. Shelf e-commerce GMV rose 49% YoY, outpacing overall platform growth.

Shein IPO stalled — FCA approved London listing but CSRC signoff not obtained; Hong Kong confidential filing as backup; valuation cut from $100B (2022) to potentially $30B: In April 2025, Shein received FCA green light for London listing but still needs CSRC approval. Shein filed confidential IPO papers in Hong Kong as a parallel path. Valuation was $100B in 2022, slashed to $66B in 2023, and potentially as low as $30B now due to competition from Temu and rising costs.

In 12 months, Haul expanded from U.S.-only to a global ultra-low price shopping destination in 25 locations. Selection grew by 400%, exceeding 1M items under $10. Customer visits tripled since June. Amazon removed 'beta' designation and launched Amazon Bazaar standalone app for 14 new markets.

Douyin's subsidies have shifted from subsidizing consumers to subsidizing merchants, with over 19 billion yuan in merchant subsidies by end of August 2025. In 2025, Douyin E-commerce cumulatively saved merchants over 32 billion yuan in costs.
In Q4, total revenue grew by 12% year-over-year to reach RMB 123.9 billion, while full-year 2025 revenue increased by 10% year-over-year to RMB 431.8 billion.

Transaction services revenue (19% YoY growth) outpaced online marketing services (5% YoY growth) in Q4, signaling Temu monetization acceleration: Online marketing services revenue for Q4 was RMB 60 billion (up 5% YoY), while transaction services revenue reached RMB 63.9 billion (up 19% YoY).
Net income attributable to ordinary shareholders in the quarter was RMB 24,541.0 million (US$3,509.3 million), a decrease of 11% from RMB 27,446.6 million in the same quarter of 2024.
The company ended 2025 with RMB 422.3 billion in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments as of December 31, 2025.

Co-Chairman and Co-CEO Jiazhen Zhao stated: '2026 marks the beginning of our journey into the next decade,' and 'Going into this new chapter, supply chain investment is where we will place our greatest conviction.'

Management stated they would proceed 'with an all-in attitude' and 'invest more manpower and resources, doubling down on supply chain upgrades and modernization.' They believe 'in the next three years, we will have the opportunity to rebuild PDD Holdings.'
VP of Finance Jun Liu noted: 'These investments are firm and long-term, and will inevitably affect our financial performance.'
Zhao highlighted two initiatives — free delivery to villages and New Quality Supply — as examples of foundational investments intended to support sustainable growth.
PDD has zero capital return — no dividends, no buybacks, 0% buyback yield as of Feb 2026: PDD Holdings does not pay a dividend. PDD Holdings' Buyback Yield % as of February 20, 2026 is 0.00%.

Operating cash flow declined: RMB 106.9B FY2025 vs RMB 121.9B FY2024 (12% decline): Operating cash flow was RMB 24.1 billion in Q4 and RMB 106.9 billion for the full year, compared with RMB 29.5 billion in Q4 2024 and RMB 121.9 billion for 2024.
Duoduo Maicai GMV surged 340% YoY in July 2025 after Meituan Youxuan exited, but experienced negative growth in 2024 and H1 2025: 美团优选退出后，多多买菜7月GMV同比增长340%，环比增长30%。但此前2024年和2025年上半年均为负增长。
拼多多在配送方面有明显短板。即时零售需要'分钟级配送'能力，但拼多多长期依赖第三方物流，多多买菜只能做到'次日达'，远落后于美团、饿了么、京东的'30分钟达'

PDD is building 200 instant retail warehouses in Shanghai with 30-min delivery, planned launch in first-tier cities by Aug 2025; break-even target 2026: 拼多多在上海建设200个即时零售仓库，计划在上海、广州、深圳等一线城市推出即时配送服务。多多买菜预计2026年实现盈亏平衡。
Duoduo Maicai 2024 total transaction volume exceeded RMB 20 billion, but PDD has RMB 364.5B in cash to invest: 2024年多多买菜总交易额超200亿元。拼多多2025年Q1财报显示现金及现金等价物和短期投资合计3645亿元。
2024年电商领域有效投诉总量超128万件，同比2023年上涨21.51%。双十一期间单日投诉峰值超过5000件。
拼多多、淘宝（含天猫）、京东、抖音四大电商平台投诉占电商领域总投诉量的83.3%
头部投诉商家中，拼多多回复了20%的投诉，淘宝（含天猫）只回复了5%的投诉
客服问题是电商投诉中最突出的因素，占比超过40%。货不对板和退货不退款的投诉也分别占据了15%左右
双11期间，电商平台在黑猫平台的投诉量高达183,867件，较上期增长22.72%
2025 Double 11 complaint share by platform: Douyin 28.6% (~53K), Taobao/Tmall 24.5% (~45K), Pinduoduo 18.3% (~33.6K), JD 16.1% (~29.6K): 抖音商城投诉量高居第一(28.61%)，淘宝含天猫位居次席(24.50%)，拼多多(18.27%)，京东(16.09%)
抖音商城、得物、快手商城和小红书商城的投诉回复率均在99%左右，拼多多则接近70%。京东不足30%，淘宝（含天猫）不足10%
Counterfeit goods complaints accounted for ~59% of Pinduoduo's total Heimao complaints (22,512 out of 38,274) as of March 2025: 拼多多假货投诉22,512件，占其平台总投诉量38,274件的约59%
拼多多累计投诉量已超过21万件
市场监管总局约谈电商平台，针对'平台利用仅退款规则挤压商家生存空间、助长低质低价竞争'提出具体整改要求
After refund-only policy cancellation (April 2025), Pinduoduo's malicious refund-only orders dropped 62% and merchant appeal success rate rose from 18% to 75%: 新规实施后，拼多多恶意仅退款订单下降62%，商家申诉成功率从18%上升至75%
Refund-only complaints as share of merchant complaints fell from 50.4% (Q1 2025) to 35.9% (Q3) to 37.1% (Q4 2025) — improved but not eliminated: 仅退款投诉在商家投诉中的占比从Q1 2025的50.36%降至Q3的35.85%和Q4的37.09%
Pinduoduo returned the 0.6% base technical service fee on refunded orders. Established 商家权益保护委员会 chaired by Co-CEO Zhao Jiazhen. Introduced 'abnormal order warnings' and 'low-price risk alerts.'
调查超2000名商家，7.9%的商家遭遇的仅退款率高达80%，仅1.06%表示从未遇到。拼多多商家申诉成功率不足5%。

Temu cumulative global downloads crossed 1 billion in Q2 2025 (+93% YoY). Peak US downloads: 12.4M/month (Jan 2024), declining to 7.6M/month (Aug 2024): Temu's cumulative global downloads cracked the 1 billion mark in Q2 2025, a 93% increase YoY. US downloads peaked at 12.4M in January 2024, declined to 7.6M by August 2024.
By later in 2025, Temu surpassed 530 million MAUs after a 48% year-over-year surge.

Temu quietly resumed advertising, running 1,100 active Meta ads and 700 active Google ads in the U.S., up from as few as 4 and 6 respectively in mid-April 2025. Temu jumped in App Store rankings as ad spend rebounded.

Temu holds a 2.0-2.5/5 rating on Trustpilot based on ~49K reviews, while Google Play rating is 4.5/5 based on ~11.4M reviews: Trustpilot: 2/5 (48,921 reviews). Google Play: 4.5/5 (11.4M reviews). Sitejabber: 2/5 (1,760 reviews). Trustindex: 2.4/5 (3,545 reviews).

Reviewers report 'terrible quality devices rated 5 stars', prices creeping up, 'free gift for new customers' promotion misleading, 'repetitive template messages from customer service for weeks', 'Customer Service bots are all very bad'

For comparison: Amazon.com rated 1.7/5 on Trustpilot (44,673 reviews), SHEIN has 350K+ reviews on Trustpilot: Amazon.com: 'Bad' 1.7/5 (44,673 reviews). Amazon.co.uk: 1.4/5. SHEIN: 350,062 reviews on Trustpilot.
Temu's user base in the U.S. plummeted—from nearly 34 million monthly users in May 2024 to fewer than 8 million in May 2025.
Temu started adding 'import charges' ranging between 130% and 150%, more than doubling the cost of items. A summer dress sold for $18.47 would cost $44.68 after import charges.

SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs (Feb 20, 2026, 6-3 ruling) but de minimis exemption status remains unclear; cheap import tariffs still in effect as of March 2026: Supreme Court ruled 6-3 in Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump that IEEPA tariffs exceeded presidential authority. However, de minimis exemption status unclear. Trump imposed new 10% tariff under Section 122 of Trade Act of 1974.
Temu stated 'All sales in the U.S. are now handled by locally based sellers, with orders fulfilled from within the country.' Over 75% of promoted 'lightning deals' flagged as locally shipped.

Temu global MAU reached 416.5M in Q2 2025 (+68% YoY), later surpassing 530M. EU MAU +74% YoY, LatAm +122% YoY, offsetting US decline: Q2 2025 global MAU: 416.5M (+68% YoY, +3% QoQ). EU share: 34% (+74% YoY). LatAm: 26% (+122% YoY). US share fell to 11% of global users (-28% YoY).
FY2025 revenue: RMB 431,845.7 million. Q4 2025 revenue: RMB 123,912.2 million (+12% YoY). FY2024 revenue: RMB 393.8B (implied from 10% growth).

FY2025 quarterly revenue segment breakdown shows Online Marketing Services (OMS) and Transaction Services (TS) roughly equal: Q1: OMS RMB 48.7B (+15%) / TS RMB 47.0B (+6%). Q2: OMS RMB 55.7B (+13%) / TS RMB 48.3B (~flat). Q3: OMS RMB 53.3B (+8%) / TS RMB 54.9B (+10%). Q4: OMS RMB 60.0B (+5%) / TS RMB 63.9B (+19%). FY2025 total: OMS ~RMB 217.7B / TS ~RMB 214.2B.

Transaction Services revenue accelerated sharply in Q4 2025, suggesting Temu GMV ramp: Q4 TS revenue grew 19% YoY vs Q2 flat and Q3 +10%. Q4 TS was RMB 63.9B, the largest quarter ever. TS growth accelerated from flat in Q2 to +19% in Q4.
FY2025 net income: RMB 99.4B (~$14.1B). Total costs of revenue rose 23% YoY. Q4 net income: RMB 24.5B, down 11% YoY.

FY2025 GMV growth was ~22-25% YoY, driven primarily by Temu volume rather than domestic ARPU: ECDB: 2024 GMV $655B -> 2025 GMV $817B = +24.7% growth. Decomposition: (A) Domestic Pinduoduo: 2024 est. RMB 4.5-5.0T -> 2025 est. RMB 4.5-5.2T = ~5-15% domestic growth. Goldman Sachs noted domestic GMV growth dropped below 20% in mid-2024, and revenue growth decelerated to 10% in FY2025 (from 90% in FY2024). Domestic growth was constrained by: consumer spending weakness, intense competition from Douyin/Taobao, and 100B merchant subsidy program dampening monetization. (B) Temu international: 2024 est. $70.8B -> 2025 est. $92.5B = +31% growth. Temu growth was ~70%+ in H1 plan but dragged down by U.S. tariff shock to ~31% actual. Domestic ARPU growth stalled in 2025 as PDD reinvested via 100B merchant subsidy program: PDD launched '100 billion core program' for merchant support in 2024, continuing into 2025. OMS revenue growth decelerated: Q1 +15%, Q2 +13%, Q3 +8%, Q4 +5%. This sequential deceleration in advertising revenue suggests PDD deliberately restrained monetization pressure on merchants. Marketing expenses surged 44% YoY in Q1 2025. Result: domestic take rate likely flat to slightly down vs 2024, meaning domestic GMV growth roughly tracked domestic OMS revenue growth (~10%).

2026 GMV forecast: $950B-$1.05T total (domestic $700-780B, Temu $120-130B, other intl $30-40B): DOMESTIC 2026: Modest 5-10% growth from consumer stimulus, quality upgrade initiative. China's express delivery sector managed 216.5 billion parcels in 2025, marking an 11.5% rise from the previous year and averaging over 141 parcels per person. The 180B milestone was hit by Nov 30. 2024 total: ~194B parcels (from 174B in 2023 + growth).

Pinduoduo's estimated share of China's express parcels is 35-40%, implying 76-87B parcels in 2025: Pinduoduo is the largest single source of express parcels in China due to its high-frequency, low-AOV model. Industry estimates (carrier commentary, analyst reports) suggest Pinduoduo generates 35-40% of China's express volume. At 216.5B total: 35% = 75.8B parcels, 40% = 86.6B parcels. Pinduoduo parcels x AOV RMB 50-60 = RMB 3.8-5.2T domestic GMV.

Logistics back-calculation supports domestic GMV of RMB 3.8-5.2T, consistent with other methods: 76-87B parcels x RMB 50 AOV = RMB 3.8-4.3T. 76-87B parcels x RMB 60 AOV = RMB 4.6-5.2T. Central estimate: ~RMB 4.2-4.8T domestic. This is consistent with Method 1 (RMB 3.96-4.84T) and Method 2 (domestic implied ~RMB 5.2T from ECDB).

PDD's blended take rate of ~7.6% is artificially high due to Temu's fully-managed model; domestic-only take rate is ~4.5-5%: PDD FY2025 revenue RMB 431.8B / estimated GMV RMB 5.7T = 7.6% blended. But this mixes two very different models: (1) Pinduoduo domestic: pure marketplace with 4.5-5% take rate (ads + commissions), (2) Temu international: fully-managed/semi-managed with 15-20% effective take rate (includes fulfillment margin). Domestic-only: OMS revenue RMB 217.7B / domestic GMV RMB 4.5-5.2T = 4.2-4.8% domestic take rate. This is converging toward Alibaba's ~4.5-5% but still slightly below.

By GMV, PDD is now the #2 global e-commerce platform, within striking distance of Amazon: 2025 Global GMV rankings per ECDB: Amazon $845B, Pinduoduo $817B, Douyin/TikTok $648B. PDD's combined platform is only 3.3% behind Amazon by GMV. If Temu grows 30-40% in 2026, PDD could overtake Amazon in 2026.

Temu's 2025 GMV was approximately $92.5B globally, up ~70-75% from 2024: Temu's annual sales amounted to US$92,514m (~$92.5 billion) in 2025. Temu's GMV in 2024 was ~$70.8B. LatePost reported Temu's full-year GMV target for 2025 was US$100 billion. ECDB data shows Temu as part of Pinduoduo's combined $817B GMV for 2025.

Temu H1 2025 GMV was ~$35B, implying H2 was ~$57B (heavy back-weighting due to tariff recovery): Temu achieved a global GMV of approximately $35 billion in the first half of 2025. Full-year ~$92.5B implies H2 was ~$57.5B. Q3 projected at $22-25B.

US de minimis removal caused 23% sales drop. Daily U.S. users fell 48% in May vs March 2025. Temu suspended U.S. marketing on May 1. Tariff on fully managed deliveries reduced from 120% to ~54%. By H2, GMV composition: US 31%, Europe 40%.

Temu 2026 GMV forecast: ~$120-130B with 30-40% growth, but EU de minimis removal is a risk: Growth target for 2026 is 30% (company internal). Analyst estimates suggest 35-40% growth. $92.5B x 1.35 = $125B. EU plans to remove 150 EUR threshold in 2026 (moved forward from 2028). Temu expanding semi-managed model and German warehouse hub in preparation.

METHOD 1: Revenue/Take-Rate yields FY2025 total GMV of RMB 5.4-6.5T ($750-900B): FY2025 total revenue: RMB 431.8B. Revenue splits into domestic (primarily OMS = ads) and international (primarily TS = commissions). APPROACH: (A) Domestic Pinduoduo GMV via OMS revenue. OMS revenue ~RMB 217.7B is primarily Pinduoduo domestic advertising. Domestic take rate estimated at 4.5-5.5% (up from ~3.9% in 2022, analyst consensus approaching 5.5% by 2026). Domestic GMV = RMB 217.7B / 0.045 to 0.055 = RMB 3.96T to 4.84T. (B) International Temu GMV from TS revenue. TS revenue ~RMB 214.2B includes both Temu commissions and domestic payment/fulfillment fees. Estimated Temu portion of TS: ~60-70% = RMB 128-150B. Temu take rate ~15-20% (higher than domestic due to fully managed model with fulfillment margin). Temu GMV from TS = RMB 128-150B / 0.15-0.20 = RMB 640-1000B ($90-140B). Cross-check: Third-party Temu GMV estimate $92.5B for 2025 = ~RMB 660B, consistent with lower end. COMBINED: Domestic RMB 3.96-4.84T + Temu RMB 660-1000B = RMB 4.6-5.8T ($640-810B). Adjusting for domestic TS contribution (~30-40% of TS from domestic marketplace commissions), domestic GMV estimate increases slightly. RANGE: RMB 5.0-6.0T ($700-835B).

METHOD 2: Third-party data providers estimate Pinduoduo total GMV at $817B in 2025: ECDB (ecommerce data provider) ranks Pinduoduo at $817B GMV in 2025, making it the #2 global marketplace by GMV after Amazon ($845B). ECDB shows 2024 GMV at $655-672B (scope-dependent). This implies ~22% YoY GMV growth. For 2024, ECDB had Pinduoduo at $655-715B, Douyin at $554B, JD.com at $506B. Separately, Pinduoduo domestic GMV was ~RMB 5.2T (est.) for 2024 with 15-20% growth, suggesting RMB 5.7-6.0T for 2025.

METHOD 3: App usage proxy (MAU x frequency x AOV) yields domestic GMV of RMB 4.0-5.5T: Pinduoduo domestic MAU: ~900M+ active buyers (company disclosure of 900M+ active buyers, MAU likely 700-800M based on QuestMobile). Order frequency: industry estimates 3-4x per month for active users (Pinduoduo users skew high-frequency, low-AOV). AOV: ~RMB 40-50 per order (last disclosed 2021 at RMB 40, likely risen to RMB 45-55 due to category mix shift toward higher-value goods and 'quality upgrade' initiative). CALCULATION: Conservative: 700M MAU x 3x/month x 12 months x RMB 45 = RMB 1.13T (too low — this assumes all MAU are equally active). Adjusted: Active buyers = 900M, annual purchase frequency ~60-80 orders/year (industry data for Pinduoduo-type platforms), AOV RMB 50. Domestic GMV = 900M x 70 orders x RMB 50 = RMB 3.15T. High estimate: 900M x 80 orders x RMB 55 = RMB 3.96T. NOTE: This method likely understates because (a) AOV data is stale (2021), (b) Duoduo Grocery and community group buying add high-frequency, low-AOV transactions that may not be fully captured. RANGE: RMB 3.2-4.0T domestic, plus Temu $92.5B = total $535-650B. This method produces a lower bound because the AOV inputs are outdated.

CONVERGENCE: FY2025 total GMV best estimate is $750-850B (RMB 5.4-6.1T), with domestic ~$650-750B and Temu ~$90-100B: Method 1 (Revenue/take-rate): $700-835B. Method 2 (ECDB third-party): $817B. Method 3 (App proxy): $535-650B (lower bound due to stale inputs). Triangulated range: $750-850B total. Domestic: RMB 4.5-5.5T ($625-765B). Temu: $90-100B. Central estimate: ~$800B total GMV. Growth from FY2024: ~22-25% YoY (from ~$655B in 2024 per ECDB).

As of December 31, 2025, PDD held RMB 422.3B (~$58.7B) in cash, equivalents, and short-term investments, plus RMB 104.7B in non-current deposits. Total liquid assets ~RMB 527B (~$73.2B). The company has never declared a dividend or executed a share buyback since its IPO in 2018. Management's Q4 2025 earnings call made no mention of buybacks or dividends.

PDD's capital allocation stance is the polar opposite of BABA and JD, who have aggressively returned capital to shareholders in 2024-2025: Alibaba: spent RMB 86.7B on buybacks in FY2025, RMB 88.7B in FY2024, RMB 74.7B in FY2023. Committed to $25B buyback through March 2027. Pays $2/share annual dividend (1.47% yield). JD.com: $5B buyback program with $1.5B deployed in H1 2025. PDD: $0 returned to shareholders — ever.

Co-CEO Jiazhen Zhao: '2026 marks the beginning of our journey into the next decade. Going into this new chapter, supply chain investment is where we will place our greatest conviction. We are committed to dedicating significant resources, with an all-in mindset, to drive lasting benefits to the entire ecosystem.' VP of Finance Jun Liu: 'These investments are firm and long-term, and will inevitably affect our financial performance.'

The market approved PDD's reinvestment thesis — stock rose 8.3% on earnings day despite 12% net income decline: PDD stock gained 8.26% to $106.23 on March 25, 2026, following the Q4/FY2025 earnings release showing revenue +12% QoQ but net income -11% QoQ. Momentum scanner triggered 59 alerts indicating high trading interest.

PDD FY2025 revenue grew 10% but operating profit declined 13%, signaling a deliberate margin-for-growth trade: FY2025 total revenues RMB 431,845.7M (+10% YoY). Operating profit RMB 94,624.1M (-13% YoY). Net income RMB 99,364.5M (-12% YoY). Non-GAAP operating profit RMB 102,561.0M (-13%). Q4 revenues RMB 123,912.2M (+12%), Q4 operating profit RMB 27,719.7M.

Revenue mix is now roughly 50/50 between online marketing (advertising) and transaction services (commissions/fulfillment), reflecting Temu's growing contribution: FY2025: Online marketing services & others RMB 217,783.0M (+10%), Transaction services RMB 214,062.7M (+9%). Q4 2025: Online marketing RMB 60,010.1M (+5%), Transaction services RMB 63,902.1M (+19%).

Cost of revenue surged 23% YoY in FY2025 driven by fulfillment fees, bandwidth/server costs, and payment processing — the cost of building Temu's local warehouse network: FY2025 total costs of revenues RMB 188,801.8M (+23% vs RMB 153,900.4M in FY2024). Increase mainly from increased fulfillment fees, bandwidth and server costs, and payment processing fees. For context, FY2024 CoR was up 68% YoY, so the rate of increase is decelerating.

R&D spending surged 30% to RMB 16.5B in FY2025, primarily for warehouse automation technology and increased headcount: FY2025 R&D expenses RMB 16,496.2M (+30% from RMB 12,659.4M in FY2024), primarily due to increase in staff-related costs and bandwidth and server costs. R&D as % of revenue rose from ~3.2% to ~3.8%.

FY2025 stock-based compensation was RMB 7.94B, with G&A (RMB 3.43B) and R&D (RMB 2.26B) as largest components: FY2025 SBC breakdown: Cost of revenues RMB 248.2M, Sales & marketing RMB 2,000.8M, G&A RMB 3,430.9M, R&D RMB 2,257.1M. Total RMB 7,937.0M. Q4 2025 SBC: RMB 1,754.4M.

In Q2 2025, PDD launched the RMB 100 billion ($14 billion) support program including: RMB 10B fee reduction for merchants, logistics subsidies, quality merchant initiatives. Components: Duoduo Premium Produce, New Quality Supply, logistics support to remote regions. Co-CEO Zhao described 2025 as 'the year in which we made our largest investment in high-quality development.'

These measures have already driven a 40% surge in orders from remote regions and improved platform sustainability. Through logistics support initiatives, the platform waived shipping fees from transit warehouses to final destination for orders shipping to remote regions.

Dedicated teams for the new quality merchant support program visited hundreds of manufacturing belts, assisting SME merchants with their transformation. In Q3 2025, the Duoduo Premium Produce team visited dozens of agriculture specialty regions to develop tailored solutions for merchants.

PDD planned to officially launch a fast-delivery retail service between August and September 2025, following a testing phase. This is considered a significant project, with an initial investment estimated at RMB 10 billion, primarily for building a delivery network and warehousing facilities. Instant retail will be PDD's sixth business unit alongside: main site, grocery shopping (Maicai), Temu, local life, and Kuaituantuan.

In 2024, Duoduo Maicai's total transaction volume exceeded RMB 20 billion. In H1 2025 it declined due to PDD's focus shift to Temu and discontinuation of financial support, requiring self-sufficiency. Following Meituan Youxuan's exit, Maicai experienced its first significant growth in July 2025, with GMV increasing by 340% YoY and 30% MoM, as it picked up former Meituan Youxuan merchants.

Among these businesses, the main site, instant retail, and Temu are considered key, primarily focused on increasing overall transaction volume. In contrast, Duoduo Maicai, local life, and Kuaituantuan are positioned as secondary businesses.

Li Lu's Himalaya Capital initiated a $482M position in PDD in Q2 2025 and held through Q4 without changes — a strong Buffett-school endorsement: Himalaya Capital opened a $482.3M position in PDD at ~$104.92/share in Q2 2025 (17.93% of portfolio). The March 20, 2026 $55.00 Call had some of the highest implied volatility of all equity options. Options activity pointed to elevated implied volatility ahead of the March 25 earnings release. PDD currently trades at 8x forward earnings per Li Lu portfolio analysis.

By October 2025, Temu operated 13 self-owned warehouses globally: 10 in Europe, 2 in the United States, and 1 near the US-Mexico border. Notable examples: 120,000 sqm 'Jitu Warehouse US-Mexico', 165,000 sqm logistics center in Gimpo, South Korea. A 14th warehouse project planned for Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.

In April 2025, the US de minimis exemption ended, meaning small-value imports were no longer tariff-free. Temu pivoted by onboarding US-based sellers, investing in regional warehouses for faster, tariff-compliant fulfillment. Temu's California warehouse (Rowland Heights) serves as West Coast hub; Dallas as central US hub. Temu cooperates with WINIT and Export for additional US warehousing capacity.

Warehouses in Rotterdam, Frankfurt, UK, Germany, Austria, Italy, Spain, and France. Projected to handle 80% of European orders domestically by 2026. EU duty reform starting July 1, 2026 will remove the EUR 150 duty-free threshold entirely, imposing a mandatory fee per item.

According to Prologis, as of Q3 2025, Chinese 3PL operators and e-commerce companies accounted for 20% of the net leasing of new warehouse space in the US, a share that has risen sharply in recent years.

PDD FY2025 owner earnings (OCF minus capex minus SBC) are approximately RMB 98B ($13.6B), implying EV/owner-earnings of ~5.7x at today's price: FY2025 operating cash flow: RMB 106,938.7M. FY2024 capex (purchase of property & equipment): RMB 967M (FY2025 capex not yet disclosed in detail; using FY2024 as proxy since quarterly CoR growth is decelerating). FY2025 SBC: RMB 7,937.0M. Owner earnings = 106,939 - ~1,000 - 7,937 = ~RMB 98,000M (~$13.6B at 7.2 RMB/USD). Market cap at $106.23/ADS: ~$150B. PDD trades at approximately 10.9x trailing P/E on FY2025 GAAP net income, vs Alibaba at ~19x forward P/E and JD.com at ~9x forward P/E: PDD FY2025 net income: RMB 99,364.5M (~$13.8B). At $106.23/ADS and ~1.4B ADS-equivalent shares, market cap ~$150B. P/E = $150B / $13.8B = ~10.9x. Alibaba forward P/E: 19.21x per analyst data. JD.com forward P/E: 9.15x per analyst data.

Cash-to-market-cap ratio of ~49% creates an extraordinary margin of safety — nearly half the equity value is backed by liquid assets: Cash + ST investments: RMB 422.3B (~$58.7B at 7.2 RMB/USD). Market cap: ~$150B. Cash/market cap = $58.7B / $150B = ~39%. Including non-current time deposits and debt securities (RMB 104.7B / ~$14.5B): total liquid assets ~$73.2B, or ~49% of market cap.

Since the beginning of July 2025, when the State Post Bureau's Party Leadership Group convened a meeting to clearly express its stance against involution, the express delivery sector has continued to surge. The average price per delivery for YTO Express and STO Express dropped to 2.19 yuan and 2.00 yuan respectively, while Yunda Express's average price per delivery fell below 2 yuan, decreasing to 1.94 yuan, a year-on-year drop of 8.6%.

The six largest privately owned express delivery companies accounted for around 82% of China's parcel deliveries by volume in the third quarter of 2024. YTO Express saw parcel volume growth of 22% year-over-year in Q2 2025, though core EBIT fell 13%.

YTO Express 2024 parcel volume ~26.6B (+25.3%), Yunda ~23.8B (+26.1%), STO ~22.7B (+29.8%). Volume growth does not translate to profit growth due to pricing pressure.: YTO Express delivered around 26.6 billion parcels (+25.33% YoY), Yunda 23.8 billion (+26.14%), STO Express 22.7 billion (+29.83%). This led to an increasingly severe phenomenon in the express delivery industry, where growth in volume does not translate to profit.
In 2025, the sector handled 216.51 billion parcels, representing a year-on-year increase of 11.8%. The industry's cumulative business revenue reached 1.8 trillion yuan, up 6.1%.

China express carrier market share rankings (2024 volume): ZTO ~34B (19.4%), YTO ~26.6B, Yunda ~23.8B, STO ~22.7B, J&T ~19.8B, SF ~13.3B.: ZTO led at ~34 billion parcels (market share dropped from 22.9% in 2023 to 19.4% in 2024). YTO Express ~26.6 billion, Yunda ~23.8 billion, STO Express ~22.7 billion, J&T Express ~19.8 billion, SF Express ~13.3 billion.
Average selling price for the core express delivery business increased 2.9% in Q4, driven by product mix improvements. For full-year 2025, ASP declined slightly by 1.7%.
J&T Express Hits Record 30 Billion Parcel Volume in 2025 with 22.2% YoY Growth.

EU countries agreed to impose a €3 tax on all parcels under €150 entering the bloc, taking effect from July 1, 2026. The €3 duty is charged per item based on tariff classification, not per parcel. From November 2026, the duty is due to be accompanied by the introduction of handling fees at two euros per parcel.

4.6 billion retail parcels arriving last year, more than 145 per second, with 91% originating from China. The EU estimates that up to 65% of low-value parcels are undervalued on customs declarations to fraudulently qualify for duty exemptions, costing member states approximately €1 billion annually.

The €3 duty will remain in place until a permanent solution is implemented, namely the planned abolition of the €150 customs duty exemption once the EU Customs Data Hub is operational. Current projections suggest the hub will be ready by 2028.
To support this transformation, Pinduoduo is building 200 instant retail warehouses in Shanghai and plans to integrate Duoduo Maicai's existing facilities.

PDD planned to officially launch a fast-delivery retail service between August and September 2025, following a testing phase. This is considered a significant project, with an initial investment estimated at RMB 10 billion, primarily for building a delivery network and warehousing facilities.

Meituan announced in June 2025 that it would shut down or suspend Meituan Youxuan operations across most of China. Following Meituan Youxuan's exit, Duoduo Maicai experienced its first significant growth in July 2025, with GMV increasing by a staggering 340% year-over-year and 30% month-over-month.
Before the end of 2025, it expects to cover all provincial capitals and first-tier cities, excluding the capital of Tibet, and aims to achieve 20 million daily transactions.
In 2024, Duoduo Maicai's total transaction volume exceeded RMB 20 billion, but its recent performance has been disappointing, with negative growth in both 2024 and the first half of 2025.

PDD plans to establish a local lifestyle business group that integrates multiple consumer-related businesses. Pinduoduo rebranded its parcel collection service under Duoduo Maicai as 'Duoduo Station', offering both pick-up and home delivery.

Pinduoduo generates 33% of all parcels shipped in China, surpassing Taobao/Tmall's share in 2023. With 216.5B industry parcels in 2025, this implies ~71B parcels attributable to PDD's platform.: Pinduoduo currently generates 33% of packages shipped in China. When Pinduoduo's parcel volume surpassed that of Taobao/Tmall in 2023, Alibaba began repositioning parcel volume as a key performance metric.
Pinduoduo now holds approximately 27-30% of China's e-commerce GMV — up from near zero in 2016.
In some agricultural regions, Duoduo Stations now account for over 25% of total parcel volume.

The transaction volume of the semi-managed model accounted for 20% of Temu's GMV in 2024. TEMU now prioritizes semi-managed products in both homepage exposure and search results. According to internal estimates, up to 70% of platform traffic is shifting toward semi-managed SKUs.
Following the de minimis exemption's end, U.S. site traffic dropped 33% and daily GMV fell 50%, though semi-managed orders now represent 80% of sales.
It is projected that by 2025, Temu's self-operated warehouses in the US will handle 15% to 20% of total US goods volume, and this proportion may increase to 20% to 25% by 2026.
Sources close to Temu's decision-makers revealed that 'in the next six months, semi-managed services will match or even surpass fully-managed services in monthly sales.'

On February 20, 2026, the Court ruled 6-3 that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) does not give the president authority to impose tariffs — invalidating the 'reciprocal tariffs,' 'fentanyl tariffs,' and virtually every new tariff imposed since February 2025.

The day of the ruling, Trump signed three executive actions: one terminating IEEPA tariffs, one imposing a new 10% global 'temporary import surcharge' under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, and one continuing the suspension of the de minimis exemption. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed July 4, 2025, permanently repeals the statutory basis for de minimis effective July 1, 2027.
China and Hong Kong lost de minimis treatment on May 2, 2025. All remaining countries lost it on August 29, 2025. At their peak, more than 4 million de minimis packages entered the U.S. daily.

The U.S. effective tariff rate remains the highest since 1946, the $800 de minimis exemption is gone for all countries, and sellers sourcing from China still face cumulative rates that can exceed 50%.

Temu US daily package volume dropped from ~1 million/day (late 2023-2024) to ~250,000-300,000/day after de minimis elimination, a ~70% decline.: Temu and Shein were each shipping an estimated one million packages a day on average in the U.S. (ShipMatrix, late 2023). In July 2024 alone, each provided carriers with about 900,000 packages daily. Temu's marketplace platform now ships roughly 250,000-300,000 packages daily, covering electronics, home goods, and consumer products.
Total U.S. domestic parcel market revenue reached $196 billion, increasing 4.1% annually, with volume growing 0.4% to 23.9 billion parcels — a new all-time high.

Temu delivery times have shifted to a 3-tier model: Express Local Warehouses (2-5 days), Semi-managed US sellers (5-10 days), and China-direct (10-25 days for remaining items).: Temu's 2026 model operates across three distinct fulfillment tiers: Express Local Warehouses in Nashville TN, Leipzig Germany, Osaka Japan, Sydney Australia delivering within 2-5 business days; standard shipping 6-20 days; express options 4-9 days. Items labeled 'Local Warehouse' ship fastest. Stockouts at domestic facilities cause 3-5 additional days delay.

By October 2025, Temu operated 13 self-owned warehouses globally, including 10 in Europe, 2 in the United States, and one near the US-Mexico border. The 14th self-owned warehouse project will be located in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.

Temu's California warehouse has been strategically positioned in Rowland Heights to serve as the West Coast distribution hub. The Temu warehouse in Dallas functions as the central US distribution hub. The company has been expanding its warehouse network to include fulfillment centers in California, Georgia, and Texas.
Ahead of the May 2 end to the U.S. de minimis exemption, the company pre-shipped large volumes of popular items by sea and stocked three months of inventory in U.S. hubs.

Rotterdam, Netherlands, and Frankfurt have been established with maximum floor space and advanced integration. Warehouses positioned in the UK, Germany, Austria, Italy, Spain, and France are projected to handle 80% of European orders domestically by 2026.
The company reports that bulk shipping to UK third-party warehouses has reduced per-item logistics costs by 40-60 percent compared to traditional air freight, while improving delivery speeds.

ZTO Express FY2025 parcel volume was 38.52 billion, up 13.3% YoY, missing its own 20-24% growth guidance of 40.8-42.2 billion parcels.: ZTO reported a full-year parcel volume of 38.52 billion parcels in 2025, a 13.3% year-over-year increase. The company had targeted a volume of between 40.8 billion and 42.2 billion parcel deliveries for 2025, representing 20% to 24% growth.
In Q4 2025, ZTO reported parcel volume of 10.56 billion, up 9.2% year-over-year. Total revenue rose 12.3% to CNY 14.5 billion in the fourth quarter.
Gross margin down 6.0 points to 25.0% for 2025. Adjusted net income was CNY 9.5 billion for the full year.

ZTO has given a guidance of growth between 10%-13% for 2026. Despite core adjusted EBITDA margins declining 650 basis points year-over-year, the company emphasized higher shareholder returns with a new $1.5 billion buyback program and targeting aggregate annual returns of no less than 50% of prior year adjusted net income.
ZTO's market share dropped from 22.9% in 2023 to 19.4% in 2024, falling below the 20% mark for the first time since 2020. ZTO sacrificed some market share in exchange for better profits.

On Jan 1, 2026, new round of state subsidies for 'large-scale equipment renewal and trade-in of consumer goods' officially launched. Central government allocated 62.5 billion yuan in special funds covering automobiles, home appliances, digital products, and livelihood infrastructure. First time policy unified and systematically promoted nationwide since 2024-2025 pilots. Subsidies connect directly to e-commerce platforms including JD.com, Tmall, Suning, and Pinduoduo via 'National Subsidy Zone' (国补专区). Subsidy automatically deducted at payment — no additional application required.

2026 purchase subsidy notice clarified: subsidy cap for each home appliance is 1,500 yuan (down from 2,000 yuan in 2025). Each digital/smart product subsidy capped at 500 yuan. 2026 policy requires Level 1 energy-efficient home appliances only. Trade-in recycling value calculated separately from national subsidy — effectively stackable.

During 618 2025, iPhone 16 Pro 128GB on Pinduoduo 百亿补贴 channel cost 5,398 yuan after stacking national subsidies — reduction of 2,601 yuan from official price of 7,999 yuan (32.5% discount). iPhone 16 Pro Max 256GB at 7,558 yuan on Taobao; basically same price on PDD. JD.com self-operated flagship 'a bit conservative in terms of price.' The 国补 subsidy and platform 百亿补贴 subsidy stack independently — consumers get both.

Domestic product-level price comparison: PDD generally cheapest on most categories except Apple/3C where JD is competitive: 618 2025 百亿补贴 pricing across platforms:

| Product | Pinduoduo 百亿补贴 | Taobao 百亿补贴 | JD.com 百亿补贴 | Official Price |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------|----------------|----------------|
| iPhone 16 Pro 128GB (w/ 国补) | ¥5,398 | ~¥5,400-5,500 | Higher | ¥7,999 |
| iPhone 16 Pro Max 256GB | ~¥7,558 | ¥7,558 | 'Conservative' | ¥9,999 |
| iPhone 16 Pro Max 512GB | ~¥9,548 | ¥9,548 | Higher | ¥11,999 |
| iPhone 14 128GB (百亿补贴 era) | ¥4,899 | ¥4,899+ | ¥4,899 | ¥5,999 |

Key findings:
- PDD often undercuts JD by as little as ¥1, ¥0.10, or even ¥0.01 on identical products
- For Apple products specifically, JD Apple Zone generally most competitive of three platforms — but gaps often just dozens of yuan
- Outside 3C digital, JD 百亿补贴 prices mostly not as competitive as Taobao and PDD
- PDD 百亿补贴 has price advantage in most categories, losing to Taobao only in rare limited-time coupon deals

Pinduoduo showed more targeted pricing, often beating JD by as little as 1 yuan, 0.1 yuan, or even 0.01 yuan on identical products. The practice of algorithmically matching and marginally undercutting is exactly what the April 2026 SAMR regulation targets. PDD's commission rate is also lowest: 0.6% on main site, 1-3% for 百亿补贴 items, vs higher rates at JD and Taobao — enabling thinner margins.

Platform differentiation beyond price: JD wins on speed (next-day delivery) and authenticity (self-operated), PDD wins on price, Taobao in between: Platform comparison summary:
- PDD: Usually lowest price, 3-7 day shipping, verified genuine (百亿补贴), 0.6% commission, improving after-sales
- JD: Competitive on Apple/3C, next-day delivery (self-operated via JD Express), self-operated = guaranteed authentic, highest commission (8-10% take rate), best after-sales
- Taobao: Middle ground on price, ~3 day shipping, varies by store on authenticity, 3-5% take rate, average after-sales

For identical branded products across 百亿补贴 programs, price differences have narrowed to single-digit yuan. PDD maintains slight edge on most non-Apple categories.

Platform AOV hierarchy: JD.com (highest) > Taobao/Tmall > Douyin > Pinduoduo (lowest); PDD ~60-65% of orders under RMB 30: AOV ranking remains JD > Taobao/Tmall > PDD. Approximately 60-65% of PDD orders are under RMB 30, vs 20-30% for Alibaba and JD. PDD primarily promotes products in the 1-200 yuan range vs Taobao/JD in the 200-1000 yuan range — forming differentiated ('staggered') competition. JD maintains advantage in 3C digital and home appliances (high AOV categories) through self-operated system, warehousing, and logistics.

2025 Double 11: total GMV RMB 1.619T (+14% YoY), average AOV RMB 279; market shares: Taobao/Tmall >50%, JD 18.9%, PDD 14.8%, Douyin 12.2%: 2025 Double 11 total online sales: RMB 1.619 trillion, +14% YoY, 5.798 billion packages, average order value RMB 279.21. Platform user scale on Nov 11: Taobao 508M (+4.2% YoY), PDD 414M (+2.5%), JD 227M (+9.8%). Market share: Taobao/Tmall dominant with >50%, JD second at 18.9%, PDD and Douyin at 14.8% and 12.2% respectively, narrowing gap with JD.

With platform user counts plateauing, GMV growth now driven by raising AOV and purchase frequency rather than user acquisition. Even PDD, built on low prices, launched 'New Brand Plan' in 2018 to introduce branded goods and raise ARPU. 百亿补贴 Phase 1 targeted high-AOV branded goods (Apple, Dyson) specifically to reach Tier 1-2 city users. Both PDD and Douyin are increasing investment in high-AOV 3C digital categories as a fast path to GMV growth.

2024 GMV estimates: Taobao/Tmall ~RMB 8T, PDD ~RMB 5.2T, JD ~RMB 4.5T, Douyin ~RMB 3.5T: From GMV data, 2024: Taobao/Tmall Group ~RMB 8 trillion, Pinduoduo ~RMB 5.2 trillion, JD.com ~RMB 4.5 trillion, Douyin ~RMB 3.5 trillion. Douyin's full-year GMV projected to exceed RMB 4 trillion in 2025, surpassing JD and approaching PDD.

29-article regulation jointly issued by NDRC, SAMR, and Cyberspace Administration of China. Takes effect April 10, 2026. Key prohibitions: (1) platforms cannot leverage dominant scale to impose 'lowest price' agreements on merchants, (2) cannot use traffic throttling, search ranking demotions, or algorithm penalties to pressure merchants into predatory price-cutting, (3) cannot force exclusive pricing arrangements, (4) cannot set different prices based on user data without explicit consent, (5) must clearly label advertisements in search results. Platforms must conduct self-inspections before effective date.

On February 13, 2026, SAMR released finalized Antitrust Compliance Guidelines for Internet Platforms. Eight risk scenarios identified: algorithmic collusion, '二选一' (pick-one-from-two), '全网最低价' (lowest price across internet), excessive pricing, sales below cost, blocking/shielding, and AI-powered price manipulation. Guidelines demand platforms conduct 'targeted screening and dynamic monitoring of core algorithmic models, including pricing algorithms, recommendation systems, ranking and advertising placement strategies.'

Platforms previously forced merchants to create 'different product variants with distinct model numbers across platforms' to maintain price parity, increasing operational costs significantly. The regulation bans this practice. SAMR and MoC also separately directed PDD Holdings to fix its 'refund-only' practice. China's 2026 market regulation priorities explicitly include curbing 'cutthroat competition' — a term targeting excessive price wars that erode margins and innovation. Pinduoduo's pricing algorithm that auto-matched or undercut competitor prices by fractions of a yuan is the exact behavior these rules target.

On Feb 20, 2026, SCOTUS held in Learning Resources v. Trump that IEEPA does not authorize unilateral tariffs (6-3 decision, Roberts writing). IEEPA invalidation cut effective rate on Chinese imports by almost two-thirds. Average effective tariff rate fell from 16.0% to 9.1% (still highest since 1947). However, SCOTUS was silent on whether ruling applied to de minimis exemption — the longstanding rule allowing packages <$800 duty-free. Trump had suspended de minimis in summer 2025, and not much changed after ruling for cheap items ordered from abroad.

After SCOTUS ruling, Trump imposed 10% tariff under Section 122 of Trade Act of 1974 (and suggested raising to 15%). Critical constraint: Section 122 tariffs expire automatically after 150 days — by July 24, 2026 — unless Congress votes to extend. Section 301 tariffs on China remain active. Tax Policy Center estimated replacing IEEPA with Section 122 reduces tariff costs on apparel/leather from 15% to 8% relative to production costs.

Despite SCOTUS ruling, 'not much changed after the ruling for cheap items ordered from abroad' per Marketplace.org. De minimis exemption question unresolved. Consumers continued ordering from Temu/Shein because 'even with tariffs, it's often cheaper than shopping domestically.' Remaining tariffs imply consumer price increase of 0.6% (~$800/household). Post-substitution: 0.5%, $600 loss per household.

At least two dozen of Temu's best-selling products tracked by Bloomberg saw prices drop by 18% on average in early September 2025 compared with late April, with some items experiencing cuts reaching 60%. Temu eliminated import fees that previously increased checkout costs significantly. The comeback followed US sales plunging >30% in June and continuing to fall >10% in July-August.

Temu halted shipping products from China directly to US shoppers in May 2025 after de minimis elimination. Items shipped directly from China were labeled as out of stock. Temu shifted to only display listings for products shipped from US-based warehouses. The company is constructing a comprehensive logistics network including cross-border shipments, warehouse storage, and last-mile delivery.

Temu added import charges of 130-150% in April-May 2025. Examples: summer dress $18.47 -> $44.68 (142% surcharge); child's bathing suit $12.44 -> $31.12 (150% surcharge). This followed Trump's 145% tariff on Chinese imports and de minimis elimination on May 2, 2025. Daily active users dropped 58% in US. App ranking fell from top-5 to 75th in Apple Store.

Temu is 40% cheaper than Amazon on close-match products (~$13.37 savings per item) but only 1% cheaper on identical branded products: Omnisend research (Dec 2024-Jan 2025) examined 23 product categories, analyzing top 25 best-selling products in each. Close matches on Temu are 40% cheaper on average, saving ~$13.37 per product. For identical/branded products, price gap is minimal (1%). Products classified as: matches (77%), perfect matches (10%), global brand products (13%). Exception: Automotive products cost 12% less on Amazon than Temu.

Temu uses more aggressive discounting: 65% of listings discounted vs Amazon's 47%; max discount 98% vs Amazon's 67%: Temu offers discounts on 65% of listings vs Amazon's 47%. Maximum discounts reach 98% on Temu vs 67% on Amazon. Fashion & Beauty is Temu's strongest category with nearly 94% product overlap and ~40% lower pricing. Trust gap remains: average Amazon product has ~50,000 reviews vs Temu's ~1,500.

Post-tariff Temu pricing: prices rose 130-150% in May 2025, then recovered 18% by Sept 2025; SCOTUS ruling reduced but did not eliminate tariff burden: Timeline: (1) Pre-May 2025: Temu 40% cheaper than Amazon on generic goods. (2) May 2025: Import surcharges of 130-150% added, more than doubling prices. US DAU dropped 58%. (3) Sept 2025: Best-sellers cut 18% avg (up to 60%) as Temu shifted to US warehouse fulfillment. (4) Feb 2026: SCOTUS struck IEEPA tariffs, cutting effective Chinese tariff rate by ~2/3. But de minimis exemption unresolved. (5) Mar 2026: 10% Section 122 replacement tariff in effect (expires July 2026). Net: Temu pricing partially recovered but not to pre-tariff levels for cross-border items.

NDRC, SAMR, and CAC published 'Rules for Price Behavior on Internet Platforms' effective April 10, 2026, with 5-year validity through April 10, 2031. Article 15 prohibits price discrimination based on personal information analysis. Platforms banned from using data, algorithms, or platform rules to enforce lowest-price agreements. Traffic throttling and search ranking demotions to pressure merchants into predatory pricing are prohibited.

January 19, 2026: Bloomberg reported China broadened probe into PDD, dispatching 100+ regulators from SAMR and STA. Investigation partially triggered by physical violence between PDD employees and SAMR inspectors. Shanghai tax authority fined PDD subsidiary (Shanghai Xunmeng Information Technology) RMB 100,000 for failing to report Q3 2025 tax information on time. Shares surged 8% on clarity of modest fine.

Livestreaming E-commerce Supervision and Administration Measures took effect January 8, 2026, jointly issued by SAMR and CAC. Require real-name registration, credit grading system for marketers, ban on false advertising and selling illegal goods. Agencies representing livestreamers must standardize recruitment and product selection. Draft issued June 10, 2025.

SAMR directed PDD to fix its refund-only practice (Bloomberg). Major platforms including Taobao, Pinduoduo, and JD fully scrapped the controversial 'refund-only' policy which allowed consumers to request refunds without returning goods after delivery.

Revisions to the Anti-Unfair Competition Law (AUCL), effective since October 15, 2025, introduce 'anti-involution' measures, including explicitly prohibiting platforms from coercing merchants to sell below cost.

EU will introduce flat €3 ($3.50) per package customs charge on e-commerce parcels valued under €150, effective July 1, 2026. EU also plans to enact €2 customs handling fee in November 2026. France, Belgium, Germany, Luxembourg, and Netherlands pushing for July 1, 2026 adoption to synchronize with €3 fee. Interim until 2028 Customs Data Hub rollout.

EC estimates approximately 4.6 billion parcels valued under €150 entered the EU in 2024, more than 90% from China. Over 65% were undervalued or split into multiple packages to stay under the €150 threshold, resulting in approximately €1 billion annual revenue loss.

France passed legislation imposing eco-tax starting at €5 in 2026, rising to €10 by 2030, capped at 50% of retail price. Additional packaging tax of €2-€4 for light packages under 2kg from outside EU. Complete ban on advertising ultra-fast fashion starts January 2026. Approved by French Senate 337-1 on June 10, 2025.

Temu designated as VLOP with 45M+ monthly EU users. Now claims 92M monthly active users. EC opened formal proceedings October 2024. In July 2025 the Commission preliminarily found Temu in breach of DSA for not properly assessing risks of illegal products. Mystery shopping exercise found consumers very likely to find non-compliant products.

If confirmed to be in breach, the EU can fine Temu up to six percent of its total worldwide annual turnover. Despite dozens of ongoing investigations and multiple preliminary findings, the Commission has not yet issued a single DSA fine.

On 10 December 2025, the EC reportedly raided Temu's premises in Ireland, as part of a probe into the company's possible non-EU subsidies. The Temu raid is the second of its kind. Court of Justice confirmed EC authority to request data stored outside EU, including on servers in China.

Under Article 52(2) of the FSR, by 14 July 2026, the EC is due to publish an FSR Report on its implementation and enforcement. Depending on findings, the EC may propose legislative changes to the FSR.

Temu is building physical infrastructure, operating 13 self-owned warehouses globally (10 in Europe, 2 in the US, 1 in Mexico). By 2025, Temu's self-operated warehouses in the US handle 15-20% of total US goods volume, projected to increase to 20-25% by 2026.

Temu's user base in the U.S. plummeted from nearly 34 million monthly users in May 2024 to fewer than 8 million in May 2025. Sales at Temu fell 17% year-on-year in the week following tariff hikes (Bloomberg Second Measure).

An insider points out that direct mail is taxed at retail, while inventory sent to overseas warehouses is taxed at wholesale. This is a key cost advantage. Semi-managed model: storage rate $0.12 per cubic foot per day, order fulfillment ~$0.85 per order.
Growth target for 2025 set between 40% and 60%, and 30% growth expected in 2026. Global GMV reached $35B in H1 2025, with 60% growth from non-U.S. markets like Europe and Southeast Asia.

Temu's low-price strategy has been affected to a certain extent, but even if the price increases by 10% to 17%, its overall price advantage still exists. Temu's shift to semi-managed logistics and 60% price cuts stabilized U.S. sales after tariffs dropped to 54% in July 2025.

May 2, 2025: de minimis exemption eliminated for products of China and Hong Kong, packages face 54% ad valorem duty. July 30, 2025: Executive order suspending de minimis treatment for ALL countries effective August 29, 2025. Permanent statutory repeal under One Big Beautiful Bill Act takes effect July 1, 2027.
The President's Executive Order affirmed that the termination of duty-free de minimis treatment for all countries remains in effect. De minimis was suspended by separate executive action, not IEEPA.

Approximately 4 million parcels entered the United States each day under Section 321, adding up to about 1.36 billion packages annually. The volume jumped from 220 million in 2016 to over a billion, with Chinese e-commerce platforms like Shein and Temu accounting for roughly 600,000 daily shipments alone.

Postal shipments are subject to new duty rates of $80-$200 per package based on country tariff levels. The specific duty methodology option will cease to be available six months after August 29. From February 28, 2026, forward, only the ad valorem duty methodology may be used.

In Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump, 607 U.S. ___ (2026), the Supreme Court held 6-3 that IEEPA does not authorize the president to unilaterally set tariffs. Chief Justice Roberts wrote majority, joined by Sotomayor, Kagan, Gorsuch, Barrett, Jackson. Thomas, Kavanaugh, Alito dissented.

Since April 2, 2025, the United States had collected an estimated $75-$85 billion in duties tied specifically to IEEPA actions. China took the heaviest hit, including a short window when rates escalated to a staggering 125%.

The decision does not affect existing tariffs on Chinese goods imposed under Section 301 of the Trade Act or Section 232 of the Trade Extension Act. Section 301 exclusions for 164 products extended until November 10, 2026.

President Trump invoked Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, imposing a temporary 15% global import surcharge effective February 24, 2026. Section 122 tariffs must end in 150 days (July 24, 2026) unless Congress votes to extend them. The administration cannot extend Section 122 unilaterally.

Net tariff rate on typical Temu product NOW: Section 301 (7.5-25%) + Section 122 (15%) + MFN duty = approximately 30-45% effective rate. Pre-SCOTUS was 54-145%.: Due to the multitude of existing duties, the effective tariff rate on many Chinese goods shipped to the US remains close to 30 percent. IEEPA invalidation cut the effective rate on Chinese imports by almost two thirds. Section 122 stacks on top of Section 301 tariffs, meaning some importers face combined rates exceeding 40%.

USTR launched two Section 301 probes covering 60 different economies. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent predicted that by August, U.S. tariffs would return to the levels in effect before the Supreme Court's ruling.
